front: How do BigQuery materialized views differ from regular views, and what are their limitations?
back: Regular views are saved SQL queries with no stored data; they execute fully on each query and bill for all underlying bytes scanned. Materialized views pre-compute and cache results, refreshing automatically or on a schedule, and queries can be served from the cache at lower cost. Limitations: materialized views support only a subset of SQL (no JOINs across datasets in some cases, no non-deterministic functions), and they count toward dataset storage costs.
